_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against javascript.info and Eloquent JavaScript. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: Modules
summary: Split a program into files with explicit exports and imports.
refresher: A module exposes selected values with `export` and uses another module with `import`. Named imports must match their exported names. Browser module scripts use `type="module"` and have their own top-level scope.

blocks.body: As a program grows, one file becomes difficult to scan and easy to break. Modules let each file own one focused responsibility while declaring exactly which pieces other files may use.

An export is a public doorway, not a copy of every variable in the file. An import creates a live connection to that exported binding. The relative path begins with `./` when the module sits beside the importing file.

blocks.caption: The exporting file defines one reusable operation, and the importing file names the same exported binding.
A named export uses braces when imported. A default export uses the brace-free form, so the two styles must agree.

blocks.body: When app.js loads, JavaScript first resolves math.js, evaluates its top-level declarations once, and then gives app.js access to `double`. Module files use strict mode automatically and do not add their top-level names to the global object.

blocks.title: Paths are part of the contract
blocks.body: A correct export can still fail to load when its path is wrong. Relative browser imports normally include the file extension, and a server must deliver the module with an appropriate JavaScript content type.

blocks.prompt: Which syntax imports a named export called `add`?
blocks.why: Named imports use braces around the exact exported name and a from clause containing the module path.

blocks.prompt: How often is a module normally evaluated when imported by several files?
choices: Once, then its exports are reused
blocks.why: The module system evaluates one module instance and shares its exported bindings with the files that import it.

blocks.title: Modules checklist
items: Give each module one clear responsibility.
Export only values other files need.
Match named and default import styles.
Check relative paths and file extensions.
